one_line_ask: Preflight-audit the supplied Torqex vector logo pack (.ai/.eps master, SVG, PDF, plus one-colour and reverse variants) against the manufacturer's nameplate print spec and return a marked-up, file-and-region-referenced findings report listing every open path, RGB colour, un-outlined text run, stray point, hairline stroke and background/bleed defect with the exact rule it breaks and a severity.
brief: Torqex Industrial is a fictional maker of hydraulic torque tools and machinery, and we are about to send our brand mark out to a nameplate vendor to be laser-etched onto anodized-aluminium equipment plates and screen-printed onto tool cases. A previous contractor delivered our 'Torqex' logo as a vector pack, but before it goes to the etching/print house we need a fresh set of eyes to confirm the artwork is genuinely production-ready — the last vendor rejected a batch because a logo file had live text and an RGB blue that shifted on the plate, and we cannot afford another rejected run. This is an AUDIT, not a redesign: do NOT redraw, restyle, re-colour or re-letter anything. You will receive two things. (1) torqex_logo_pack: the delivered artwork — torqex_master.ai (the editable vector master), torqex_master.eps and torqex_master.pdf (the same mark exported), torqex_logo.svg (the web export), plus torqex_onecolour_black.ai (single-colour knockout) and torqex_reverse_white.ai (reverse/knockout-on-dark variant). (2) torqex_nameplate_print_spec.pdf, the vendor's print-spec sheet stating the rules the pack must satisfy: all colours must be CMYK (or spot) — no RGB; the approved brand col
